task_completion.description: Access the Muncie HEC-RAS model data (specifically the HDF5 output file).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a script, notebook, or tool opening or referencing the Muncie.p04.hdf file.

task_completion.description: Extract Main Channel, Left Overbank, and Right Overbank velocities at the peak flow timestep.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or tool interface showing the extraction of velocity data for the channel and both overbanks specifically at the peak flow profile/timestep.

task_completion.description: Calculate the lateral velocity differential (shear) between the main channel and the overbanks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or spreadsheet formulas computing the difference between the main channel velocity and the left/right overbank velocities to find the maximum shear difference.

task_completion.description: Export the results to lateral_shear_analysis.csv with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal commands, script execution, or file explorer showing the creation of 'lateral_shear_analysis.csv' in '~/Documents/hec_ras_results/' with columns: River_Station, Vel_Channel, Vel_Left, Vel_Right, and Max_Shear_Diff.

task_completion.description: Save a summary of the top 3 River Stations with the highest shear differential to high_shear_zones.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code sorting the results to find the top 3 stations and saving them to 'high_shear_zones.txt' in '~/Documents/hec_ras_results/', or the final text file being displayed/verified.

integrity.description: The agent actually calculated the shear differentials using a script or tool.
